title: Contract Labour License Online in India | Apply with Professionals Law
mainHeading: Contract Labour License Registration Online in India

hero.headline: Ensure Legal Hiring of Contract Workers
hero.subtext: Comply with the Contract Labour (Regulation & Abolition) Act, 1970 with Professionals Law’s expert support.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The employment of contract labour in India is regulated under the Contract Labour (Regulation and Abolition) Act, 1970, which ensures that contract workers are not exploited and are provided with fair wages, proper working conditions, and statutory benefits. Any principal employer or contractor engaging 20 or more contract workers (in most states) must obtain a Contract Labour License from the relevant Labour Department.

Professionals Law Private Limited specializes in providing end-to-end advisory, documentation, and registration services for Contract Labour License, ensuring total compliance with statutory obligations under labour laws.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Contract Labour License is legally required to regulate the employment of contract workers and ensure their welfare. Without the license, hiring contract workers becomes unlawful and can result in legal penalties.
Professionals Law Private Limited ensures businesses operate within legal boundaries while employing contract labour and stay protected from legal risks.

body.title: Legal Compliance
body.description: Obtaining the license ensures full compliance with the Contract Labour Act, protecting both employer and workers.

body.title: Employer Protection
body.description: The license shields employers from legal action and labour disputes related to unauthorized employment of contract workers.

body.title: Worker Welfare
body.description: It ensures fair wages, proper work conditions, and statutory benefits to contract workers, promoting industrial harmony.

body.title: Business Continuity
body.description: Lawful employment of contract labour ensures smooth business operations and helps avoid work stoppages due to legal non-compliance.

body.title: Credibility with Government and Clients
body.description: Licensed contractors and employers enjoy greater trust and easier approvals from government bodies and clients.

criteria: Principal Employers: Any establishment employing 20 or more contract workers through contractors (subject to state-specific applicability thresholds).
Contractors: Any contractor supplying 20 or more contract workers to any establishment.
Must be registered with the local Labour Department.
Should comply with the prescribed welfare, safety, and employment standards under the Act.

documents: Registration under Shops and Establishment Act / Factories Act
Identity and address proof of employer/authorized signatory
Board resolution or authorization letter for filing application
Estimated number of contract workers engaged
Copy of principal employer registration certificate
Copy of contract agreement with principal employer
Identity proof and address proof of contractor
Copy of ESI & EPF registration (if applicable)
Challan for license fees and security deposit

body.description: Professionals Law’s experts assess your specific requirement and verify applicable laws as per your location and industry.
Gathering and validating all necessary documents from both principal employer and contractor.
Drafting and preparing the online/offline application as per respective state labour department’s portal.
Government fees and security deposits are calculated based on the number of contract workers.
Filing of application with the concerned Labour Commissioner’s Office or online portal.
The labour department may conduct physical inspection or verification.
On successful review, the Contract Labour License is granted.
Professionals Law continues to provide support for ongoing compliance like returns filing, renewals, and audits.

penalties.heading: Penalties (If Any)
body: Monetary penalties ranging from ₹500 to ₹1000 per day for each default.
Suspension or cancellation of the license.
Legal action, prosecution, and even imprisonment.
Debarment from engaging contract workers in the future.
Liability for compensation claims from workers or their representatives.

body.question: Who is required to obtain Contract Labour License?
body.answer: Both principal employers and contractors engaging 20 or more contract workers (subject to state rules) must obtain registration and license.

body.question: Can a contractor work without a license?
body.answer: No, employing contract labour without a valid license is a legal violation and invites penalties.

body.question: Does the license need to be renewed?
body.answer: Yes, Contract Labour License is valid for a specified period and must be renewed before expiry.

body.question: Are there state-specific rules for Contract Labour License?
body.answer: Yes, rules and applicability thresholds may differ slightly from state to state.

body.question: What is the security deposit for Contract Labour License?
body.answer: The security deposit is usually calculated on a per-worker basis, varying between ₹90 to ₹1000 per worker depending on the state.

body.question: Is inspection mandatory for issuing license?
body.answer: In many cases, labour authorities conduct a physical verification of premises and records before granting the license.

body.question: Can one contractor hold multiple licenses?
body.answer: Yes, but separate licenses may be required for each establishment or state depending on operations.

body.question: What if the license is not obtained?
body.answer: Operating without a license may lead to legal penalties, suspension of operations, and criminal liability.

body.question: How does Professionals Law assist in obtaining Contract Labour License?
body.answer: Professionals Law Private Limited offers complete support from eligibility verification, documentation, filing, liaison with labour authorities, and continuous post-license compliance services.
